observations.recommendation:
1.5. We recommended the Municipal Accountant to do a restatement of the
Financial Statements to correct material error and to immediately submit
to the Office of the Auditor of the updated set of Financial Statements.

1.10. We recommended the Management to make the necessary adjusting
entries to reclassify transactions to the appropriate Inventory account.
And submit the updated and corrected Financial Statements and Notes to
Financial Statements.

Therefore, we ..-ecommended foe the Management to:

a) Create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of
members to be able to complete the physical inventory. The
Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from the
Accounting and Property Units of the Municipality.
b) Coordinate with the Property Division/Unit to strategize on how to
conduct and complete the physical inventory within prescribed
period.
c) Prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) which shall be approved
by the Local Chief Executive and submit a copy the Auditor.
d) Conduct physical count of all PPE in accordance with the PIP.
e) Prepare and submit to the Auditor the Report on Physical Count
of PPE (RPCPPE)/Physical Inventory Report.
f) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer acting
as the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain subsidiary
ledgers and property ledger card for each PPE.

2.5. We recommended the head of the agency to instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in
accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and
maintain the corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers for auditor's immediate
review and verification. We further recommended that for the ensuing
months, the BRS for all accounts be submitted to the Audit Team on a
monthly basis.

3.5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should:
a) Determine if the recorded payables are duly supported with required
documents;
b) Verify if there are actual claims filed administratively or judicially;
and
c) To revert said payables if to be found that there are no valid claims,
pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445.

4.6. We recommended the Municipal Accountant:
a) To update Subsidiary Ledger for Loans Payable identifying the
Creditor/s; and
b) To submit revised Notes to Financial Statements to include details of
the Loans Payable.

5.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

a) Instruct the Municipal GSO to prepare and submit the necessary
Report on Local Road Network of the Municipality of Maluso;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an addendum on the Notes
to the Financial Statements and disclose the Total Road Network
System using the template as required under the circular; and
c) Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on
the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the Municipality.

6.5. Thus, we recommended the Management require the Accounting Division
to strictly observe the description of accounts provided in the Revised Chart
of Accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1,
2015, to ensure correctness of the expense account that should be charged
based on the nature of the transaction.

7.4. We recommend the Municipal Accountant to observe the regulation and
submit the required DRRM utilization report on or before 15th day of the
ensuing month.

7.9. We recommended the Management to submit justification as to why the
report includes programs and activities which are not in the LDRRMF
and if it was mistakenly report, the Management should submit the correct
Quarter 2022 Utilization Report of LDRMMF.

7.15. We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

a) Designate a Property Officer who shall be responsible for the
preparation and maintenance of Report of Inventory, Stock Cards
and Property Ledger Cards; and
b) Instruct the immediate submission of the reports and ledger cards to
the COA Auditor.

8.9. We recommended the Management and Municipality GFPS:

a) To strictly observe JMC Circular 2013-001, as further amended by,
JMC 2016-01 for GAD Planning and Budgeting;
b) To facilitate the timely preparation and submission of GAD Plan and
Budget and Accomplishment Reports;
c) Furnish the Audit Team duly approved GAD Plan and Budget; and
d) To submit to the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report with its the
supporting documents.

9.9. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall:

a) Lead in the creation of the Local School Board which shall be
responsible for the creation of the budget and disbursement of funds;
b) Furnish the Audit Team the Executive Order creating the Local
School Board; and
c) LSB to comply with the laws and circulars and prepare the Special
Education Fund Budget.

10.6. We recommended the Management to submit justification as to why the
report includes projects, programs and activities which are not in the
Annual Investment Plan and/or the Management should verify the
correctness of the monitoring report and resubmit the corrected
4th
Quarter 2022 PPA Monitoring Report.

10.9. We recommended the Management to submit the SAAOB for its
development fund and to timely submit the PPA Monitoring Report to
the Audit Team.

11.3. We recommended the Management to immediately submit to the Audit
Team the accomplished and updated Agency·Action Plan and Status of
Implementation CY 2021.

12.4. We reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to
direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
prepare the SCBAA as of December 31, 2022. We further recommend
that for the ensuing years, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately
to the audit team for review.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to: a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory count annually; and b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed PPELC; and c. Instruct the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit a copy to the Audit Team for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management: a) Make a representation with the authorized depository banks to provide copies of bank statements. And if the bank cannot immediately provide the bank statements, electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions for each bank account is sufficient to enable the accounting unit to update the preparation and submission of bank reconciliation statements; and b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare promptly the monthly BRS within ten (10) days after the receipt of Bank Statement

We recommend that the Municipal Chief Executive shall: a) Require the supply inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post repair inspection to properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the reports to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from date of each inspection activity; b) Direct the Municipal General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer, as the case may be, to maintain the records regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of the Municipality; and c) The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories at least semi-annually, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Management: a) The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the RIS to support every issuance of supplies and inventories to requisitioning units or end-users; prepare and submit the SSMI to the Accounting Unit as basis for the recording of related expenses; b) The Municipal Accountant, Municipal GSO and Municipal Treasurer, shall prepare and maintain complete Supply Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, conduct periodic reconciliation of their records; and update the same based on the results of the physical inventory; c) The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories at least semi-annually, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the regulation and furnish to the Audit Team a copy of the perfected contracts and the necessary supporting documents for review

We recommend that the Management: a) Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for accounts, as well as the corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs). Ensure also that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements; and b) Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant supporting documents/ reports required by established accounting standards

We recommend that the Management that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal Engineer must submit the list of all on - going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and submit monthly monitoring PPA's report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 -004.
(b) disclose the road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Chief Mayor shall instruct the concerned department heads of accounting and property to: a) Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting subsidiary records. b) Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and per fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the CIPLC shall record promptly the construction costs and other information and associated costs about the asset constructed.
We recommend that the Municipal GSO shall prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly report of fuel consumption and the Report of fuel consumption per vehicle as required

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to instruct concerned accountable officers to timely submit the necessary reports and supporting documents to substantiate the reliability and accuracy of the amounts presented in financial statements and to facilitate the timely submission to the Office of the Auditor
observations.managementAction: and b) Ensure that the submission of liquidation reports and supporting documents of the ensuing years are within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management: a) Adhere and comply with RA 9003 and create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board
observations.managementAction: b) Prepare compliant Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan
observations.reason: and d) Submit to the Audit Team a copy of the Solid Waste Management Plan and the Executive Order evidencing the creation of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall coordinate with the Local Water District (Maluso Water District) to adopt, formulate and implement Septage Management Program as required by under the law. We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive may propose to the Sanggunian the enactment of municipal ordinances to properly address the domestic wastewater discharges.

We recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) shall: A. Prepare and submit the required annual LDRRMFIP and to review the accuracy of the appropriation for pre and post disaster programs, projects and activities and particularly for the Quick Response Fund; B. Prepare and submit the Report of Utilization Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund within 15 days after the end of each month, using Annex J-1 format, as provided for under the said COA Circular.

We recommend that for ensuing years the Management shall, within the prescribed period given under the rules, prepare and submit the necessary report pertaining to GAD. We further recommend that the Management comply with COA Circular 2014 and submit a copy of the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the GAD Accomplishment to the Audit Team.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare the SCBAA as of December 31, 2021 and submit to the Audit Team. We further recommend that for the ensuing years, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for review.

We  recommended that the Municipal Accountant

a. Determine if the recorded payables are duly supported with required documents.
b. Verify  if there are actual claims filed administratively or Jjudicially.

. To  revert said payables if to be found that there are no valid claims, pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the documentary requirements of implementation and liquidation of all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.1.2 and Item 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as  of December 31, 2020 and submit to COA.

We further recommend that for CYe 20210 “Sihe SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for review.

Accountant to comply with the regulation and furnish to the Audit Team a copy of

We recommend the Management that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal
Engineer must submit the list of all on — going government
projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year
and submit monthly monitoring PPA’s report of the agency, as stated in COA

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a) Instruct the Municipal GSO to prepare and submit the necessary
Report on Local Road Network of the Municipality of Maluso;

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an addendum on the Notes
to the Financial Statements and disclose the Total Road Network
System using the template as required under the circular; and

¢) Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct the
concerned department heads of the accounting and property fo:

a) Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting
subsidiary records.

b) Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and
per fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the
CIPLC shall record promptly the consiruction costs and other

We recommend that the Municipal GSO shall prepare and submit to the Audit
Team the monthly report of fuel consumption and the Report of fuel consumption

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to instruct concerned accountable officers to
timely submit the necessary reports and supporting documents to substantiate
the reliability and accuracy of the amounts presented in financial statements and
to facilitate the timely submission to the Office of the Auditor; and

b) Ensure that the submission of liquidation reports and supporting documents of
the ensuing years are within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the Management:

a) Adhere and comply with RA 9003 and create a Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board;

b) Prepare compliant Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan;

¢) Implement such plan to ensure efficient disposal of municipality’s solid
waste; and

d) Submit to the Audit Team a copy of the Solid Waste Management Plan
and the Executive Order evidencing the creation of Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board.

We recommend that the Chief Local Executive in coordination with the Local Water
District (Maluso Water District) to adopt, formulate and implement Septage
Management Program as required by under the law. We further recommend that the
Local Chief Executive may propose to the Sanggunian the enactment of municipal
ordinances to properly address the domestic wastewater discharges.

A. Prepare and submit the required annual LDRRMFIP and to review the

projects and activities and particularly for the Quick Response Fund.:

B. Prepare and submit the Report of Utilization Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund within 15 days afier the end of each month, using

We recommend the Management to comply with COA Circular 2014 and submit a
copy of the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) CY2021 and the GAD Accomplishment to
the Audit Team.

We further recommend that for ensuing years the Management shall, within the
prescribed period given under the rules, prepare and submit the necessary report
pertaining to GAD

observations.reason: Delayed submission of the approved BGCM budget and non-submission of its revised Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for BGCM PPA’s, precluded the Auditor from conducting timely audit of the BGCM’s transactions in the Agency’s books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended that the Management strictl y adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommend to the Management to strictly observe the provisions in Scction 3.7 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS and to submit Subsidiary Ledger for the Bayanihan Grant to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.

We  recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare  the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. This is to facilitate the immediate verification so that the necessary adjusting_entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections can be made, in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.
We  recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the documentary Tequirements of all appropriate  officers before affixing his signature in Box A of the Disbursement Voucher.

We  recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the CY2020 GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken  during the year. We further recommend that for CY 2021, the GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant a. Determine if the recorded payables are duly supported with required documents. b. Verify if there are actual claims filed administratively or judicially. c. To revert said payables if to be found that there are no valid claims, pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445.
We recommend that the Mayor to: a. Require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Management: 1. Prepare its Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan which is in compliant with the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003. 2. Implement such plan to ensure efficient disposal of municipality’s solid waste 3. Create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as required by RA 9003.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2019 and submit to COA. We further recommend that for CY 2020, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for review.

Recommendation:
We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC,
likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend
further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

Management Comment:
The Municipality of Maluso assured that they were faithfully complying the said
Local Budget Circular thru the DILG full disclosure. The reports were made
quarterly and stated that these are posted in three conspicuous place like the Public
Market, Municipal Office Hall and School Building.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team did not receive any documents or photos evidencing their claim that
they have complied with the said Local Budget Circular. Additionally, there was non-
submission of the required Annex B format report submitted to the Office of the Audit
Team Leader.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend to Management to:
1. Prepare and Submit the Monthly Report of Fund Utilization and Status of
Implementation of PPAs funded from Bayanihan Grant for CY 2020; and
2. Indicate, specifically, as to the location of three (3) conspicuous public places
where the accumulated reports are posted. And a photo of the posted reports to
substantiate compliance.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA
7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental
budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

Management Comment:
The Management had submitted the (APP) at the Office of the Audit Team Leader.
The Municipality said that there was a delay in the submission of it.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Upon verification, the Office of the Audit Team Leader have not received copies of
the revised Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for BGCM PPAs and the Approved
Budget for BGCM. What the Municipality submitted was their original Annual
Procurement Plan CY2020.

Recommendation:
We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular
(LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the
Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

Management Comment:
The Municipality of Maluso stated that the Management maintained a separate
Account for Bayanihan Grant with SB Resolution.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Review of the records showed no Special Accounts Subsidiary Ledger submitted as to
the Office of the Audit Team Leader to justify the claim that they have indeed created
a separate account for the Bayanihan Grant.

This observation pertains only to a flaw in accounting and reporting systems wherein
the Management was not able to create a Special Accounts Subsidiary Ledger for the
Bayanihan Grant for easy tracking and monitoring of utilization.
Monitoring will be conducted by the Audit Team in CY 2021.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe the provisions in Section 3.7
of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of
MNGAS and to submit Subsidiary Ledger for the Bayanihan Grant to the Office of
the Audit Team Leader.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant

a. Determine if the recorded payables are duly supported with required
documents.

b. Verify if there are actual claims filed administratively or judicially.

c. To revert said payables if to be found that there are no valid claims, pursuant
to Section 98 of PD 1445.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare
the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with
COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. This is to facilitate the immediate
verification so that the necessary adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or
other reconciling items requiring corrections can be made, in order to reflect the
correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank
reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

Management Comment:
The Management informed the Audit Team that said Bank Reconciliation Statement
were submitted on a quarterly basis.

Auditor’s Rejoinder
COA Rules and regulations must be strictly adhered to. The Bank Reconciliation
Statement is an important report to serve a control measure in the efficient
management of the cash account and to prevent risk of possible misappropriation of
funds. It is still in violation of regulation because what is required is the submission
of monthly BRS monthly.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the documentary
requirements of implementation and liquidation of all funds transferred by the
National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.1.2 and Item 3.1.3
of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the
specified purpose.

Management Comment:
The Municipality of Maluso has submitted the disbursements documents including
the fund transfer to the Audit Team Leader Office.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Upon review of records, the Municipality submitted vouchers of disbursements
together with the Reports of the Checks Issued and Reports of Disbursements. But
there was no Copy of MOA/Trust Agreement, Copy of OR upon receipt of funds transferred
and Copy of OR issued by the Source Agency evidencing refund of unexpended/unutilized
balance of fund transfer presented to the Audit Team.
Monitoring will be conducted by the Audit Team in CY 2021.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 and render a copy of the lacking supporting documents.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the documentary
requirements of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the
Disbursement Voucher.

Management Comment:
The Municipality of Maluso indicated that reports was submitted to the Office of the
Audit Team Leader every quarter.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Upon verification, indeed the Disbursement Vouchers are submitted to the Office of
the Audit Team Leader, however, it can be gleaned from it that the supporting
documents are with discrepancies and/or incomplete such as the Official Receipts
amounts does not match with the DV and Purchase Order. Also, supporting
documents requiring signatures of recipients are submitted sans the signatures which
tantamount to clear violation of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001.
Implementation of the recommendations shall be properly monitored.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice
of Disallowance, upon post audit of related transactions.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001 and ensure that all claims against government funds
shall be supported with complete documentation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the CY2020
GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and
activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend that for CY 2021, the
GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that
GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the
prescribed period.

Management Comment:
The Management signified that they have submitted the Annual Report to the Office
of the Audit Team Leader.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Review of records shows that no detailed GAD Accomplishment Report CY2020 was
submitted as to the Office of the Audit Team Leader to justify the claim. What the
Management submitted was the Annual Budget for CY2020 together with the GAD
Budget CY2020.
Monitoring will be conducted by the Audit Team in CY 2021.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice
of Disallowance, upon post audit of related transactions.
We recommend to the Management to submit the Office of the Audit Team Leader
the CY2020 GAD Accomplishment Report and Approved GAD Plan CY2021.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to submit the above-
mentioned reports within the prescribed timetables so that timely post-audit of
transaction could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

Management Comment:
The Management stated that they have submitted the Report of Collection and
Deposit monthly while the Report of Checks Issued, Bank Reconciliation Statement
are submitted quarterly.

Auditor’s Rejoinder
The Audit Team is appreciative that the Management has taken an effort to submit
the required reports, however, the submission of these reports were made beyond the
prescribed periods provided for under various COA Rules and Regulations
Upon review of records, the timelines set forth in the COA Rules and Regulations
have not been followed. There is also delays in submission of reports of their
financial transactions.
Implementation of the recommendations shall be properly monitored.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice
of Disallowance, upon post audit of related transactions.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe and submit reports within
the prescribed periods consistent with various COA Rules and Regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2020 and submit to COA. We further
recommend that for CY 2021, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the
Audit Team for review.

Management Comment:
The Management has taken note of the observation and assured compliance.

We recommend that Management: a. Make a representation with the authorized Municipality to provide copies of Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository accounts. other debits or credits to the accounts. If the bank cannot immediately provide the bank statements, electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions for each bank account is sufficient to enable the accounting unit to update the preparation and submission of bank reconciliation statements. b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare promptly the monthly BRS within ten (10) days after the receipt of the Bank Statement.

We reiterate our recommendation to the Management to: a. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually; A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. b. Conduct periodic physical inventory of the LGU’s property, plant and equipment and a report relative thereto must be prepared and submitted to the Audit Team not later that January 31 of each year. c. Direct the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to likewise maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. d. Direct the Accounting Office to maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements. e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation charges for its Land Improvements and Road Networks to avoid overstatement and understatement of accounts.

We recommend that the Construction in Progress account of the Trust Fund property to: a. Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting subsidiary records. b. Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and per fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the CIPLC shall record promptly the construction costs and other information and associated costs about the asset constructed.

We recommend that the Management: 1. furnish the Audit Team with the documentary requirements of implementation and liquidation of all funds transferred by National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.1.2 and Item 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

We recommended that the Management: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and the Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and 2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management: Require the Municipal Accountant to submit Monthly, Quarterly, Semestral and Annual Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period set by the Commission.

We recommend that the Management: Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors to prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

We recommend that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the CY2019 GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend that for CY 2020, the GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the Management: 1. Adhere to the COA rules and regulations. 2. Prepare and submit a complete list of all PPA CY 2019. 3. Conduct monitoring of all LGU implemented on-going projects implemented. 4. Prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of implementation. 5. Submit Quarterly Report of PPAs to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or within the prescribed period. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

We recommended that the Management: a. Review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting from the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the transactions, posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary and general journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of financial statements to check and test whether it is in accordance with the established accounting standards, rules and regulations. Immediately correct and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed. b. Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for EACH (emphasis given) Balance Sheet and Income Statement Account, as well as Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) to those accounts/category/accounting responsibility. Ensure also that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements. c. Make sure also that the beginning balances carried forward in a particular year in the GL and SL should be the same with the ending balances in the previous years. d. Prepare General Ledger for each account presented in the Financial Statements. e. Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant supporting documents/ reports required by established accounting standards

Implement such plan to ensure efficient disposal of municipality’s solid waste 3. Create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as required by RA 9003.

statements.

appearing in the LGU financial statements.

of accounts.

Recommendation:
The Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer shall maintain stock ledger cards, property ;edger cards, WOABSLC and other subsidiary records and reconcile accounting and property records periodically;
The Inventory Committee shall conduct complete physical inventory of all property and reconcile results with accounting records and record any adjustments in the books;
The Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation on all depreciable assets and ensure the reconciliation and update of the aforementioned dormant accounts; and
The Municipal Treasurer/Property officer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of all identified unserviceable properties and facilitate their immediate disposal to prevent further deterioration and decrease in salvage values. The Municipal Accountant should effect the necessary adjustments in the PPE Account to reclassify the book value of unserviceable properties to Other Assets account pending disposal and close the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation.

It was noted during the verification of LGU’s financial statements as of December 31, 2016, it failed to submit Property Ledger Cards and Subsidiary Ledgers (all accounts).
Subsidiary Ledger (SL) is the document to property records the significant accounting events and entries for particular major or minor accounts. SL is the primary record of the history of the account. Without this document, the debit and credit totals of an account cannot be verified with assurance of reliability.
In the conversion of accounts from the old NGAS to Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) environment, the subsidiary ledgers were required because SLs contain all the significant records of transactions including journal and memorandum entries affecting the accounts, thus making the verification of the nature, cost and existence, more convenient and reliable.
Property Ledger is the document maintained by the Accounting Section to record the acquisition of an agency asset, including its origin, cost, and at later dates, the amount of accumulated depreciation, adjustment, major additions, repairs, and so on. Without recording these entries, the balance of the account cannot be reliably determined.
One of the assertions made by management is that cost of repairs and parts were carried at cost and depreciated accordingly, however, this cannot be verified due to the non-submission of the SL or the Property Ledger that should have been the repository of such entries to the books.
The failure of the agency accountant to present the SL and  Property Ledger Cards render the financial statements not PPSAS compliant.
Recommendation:
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts.
Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts.
Reconcile the Inventory Report with the Property Cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from December-November, 2016 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification.
Further it is recommended that the management shall re-evaluate their internal control on accounting and financial operations to prevent possible loss of government funds due to not PPSAS compliance.
